channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Pebblebee’s Halo can help track lost items and keep you safe, and it’s on sale for $50
item.description: If you’re planning to travel this summer, both a Bluetooth tracker and a personal safety device can come in handy, especially if you’ll be exploring on your own. The Pebblebee Halo combines those...

item.title: AI Pair Programming Isn't Autopilot: Scaffolding HandyFEM and Catching What the AI Threw Away
item.description: The agent writes the code. You're still the engineer. I'm building HandyFEM with Claude Code as my pair. It's fast — sometimes startlingly so. But the way I work with it is deliberate: I treat...

item.title: WebRTC Video Call Tutorial with PeerJs Alternative
item.description: WebRTC Video Call Tutorial: Build a 1:1 Video Chat in JavaScript with @metered-ca/peer Scope — a 2-tab, 1:1 WebRTC video call. Two browser tabs join the same channel, each publishes its camera,...

item.title: APEX: Agentic Production Execution
item.description: APEX: Agentic Production Execution Pronounced "AY-pecks" — a production-grade operating model for teams where humans design and verify while agents execute and iterate. ☝️ This is a reference...

item.title: AdaEngine 0.1.0: a Swift game engine with ECS, plugins, Metal/WebGPU, and hot asset reloading
item.description: I’ve been working on AdaEngine, an open-source game engine and app framework written in Swift, and I’ve just released version 0.1.0. The idea behind AdaEngine is to explore what a modern game...

item.title: Python Code Obfuscation: A Practical Guide to Protecting Your IP
item.description: You've built something valuable in Python. Maybe it's a proprietary algorithm, a business logic engine, or an AI application with carefully crafted prompts. Now you need to distribute it — but...

item.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
item.description: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

item.title: This could be Windows’ M1 moment — but expect it to cost a ton
item.description: Nvidia's announcement that it's getting into the consumer laptop chip space with RTX Spark is huge. Apple has proved for years that Arm-based chips can perform incredibly well while also...

item.title: How We Built a Self-Refactoring AI Agent: Inside the "Memory Garbage Collector" of Hermes
item.description: If you have ever built an autonomous AI agent, you have likely run into the "memory bloat" problem. At first, your agent is fast, sharp, and highly efficient. It solves tasks, writes code, and...

item.title: Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo
item.description: Google's new "24/7" AI agent, Gemini Spark, can be shockingly good at doing things on your behalf. But I'm not sure it's worth the financial cost and potential privacy tradeoffs. The company gave...

item.title: Microfrontends Are Deployment Boundaries
item.description: Why independent deployment is the real boundary, why build-time composition keeps teams coupled, and why projection is a better primitive for loose microfrontends. Microfrontends are usually sold...

item.title: Setting up Azure MCP with Service Principal
item.description: Today I'm going to focus on how to set up Azure MCP Server, but more specifically how to use a service principal to authenticate. During my setup, and I've done this with both MacOS using npm and...

item.title: Astro vs Next.js vs SvelteKit for content sites in 2026
item.description: Affiliate disclosure: all three frameworks are open source so there's nothing to be paid for here. Tool links go to project sites. i run content sites on all three. portfolio on Astro, SaaS...

item.title: Introducing StartupBase 2 🎉
item.description: Nine years ago, in 2017, I launched StartupBase. At the time, it was a simple side project built around a simple belief: Great products deserve a better way to get discovered. Back then, product...

item.title: Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its offical NPM channel
item.description: Anyone who has downloaded affected Red Hat packages should investigate immediately.

item.title: Meta’s own AI was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts
item.description: Meta's AI support chatbot helped hackers hijack Instagram accounts, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a video shared on Telegram, a hacker shows how they could take over an account by asking...

item.title: Casey Neistat’s guide to posting every day
item.description: Some news: The Vergecast is now a daily podcast! Starting today, we'll be posting every weekday, with even more gadgets and rankings and conversations and feelings and podcasts-within-podcasts....

item.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
item.description: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
item.description: A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a...

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after...

item.title: Anthropic Confidentially Files for What Could Be the Largest IPO Ever
item.description: The AI giant behind Claude submitted paperwork on Monday that would take it public, just a couple of weeks after SpaceX’s splashy IPO announcement.

item.title: Vulnerability Disclosure in the Age of AI
item.description: New article: “Responsible Disclosure in the Age of AI: A Call for Urgent Action,” by Melissa Hathaway. Abstract: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping the balance between...

item.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
item.description: After months of speculation about whether OpenAI or Anthropic would be first in their race to IPO, Anthropic on Monday reached a key milestone: filing to kick off the process with the U.S....

item.title: Anthropic files to go public
item.description: The company said Monday it has filed confidentially for an IPO.

item.title: Sony’s new fight stick and gaming monitor launch in August
item.description: Sony is sharing new details about some of its upcoming gaming-focused hardware, including pricing and August launch dates for its FlexStrike fight stick and its 27-inch monitor. The FlexStrike...

item.title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies
item.description: WindBorne benefits from its unique combination of model-building and data collection. The company now has about 400 balloons in flight gathering sensor readings at any given time, launched from 15...

item.title: How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026
item.description: A collage of I/O-related images, including the Antigravity Coffee Co. pop-up, a colorful jellyfish and a still from the Timmy TPU video. The word AI repeats three times on the left of the image,...

item.title: Introducing Mellum2: A 12B Mixture-of-Experts Model by JetBrains
Summer Game Fest 2026: All the news from gaming’s busiest week
item.description: Get ready for some gaming news. It’s officially June, which means splashy new events from PlayStation, Xbox, and gaming hype man Geoff Keighley. But this season doesn’t just feature the big...

item.title: An affordable, long-lasting AirTag alternative is $15 right now
item.description: There are many solid Bluetooth trackers for iPhones that tap into Apple’s expansive Find My network. Some are thin, some are a bit chunkier. And, evidently, some look like tiny soccer balls....
